channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: 007 First Light is Reportedly the Most Expensive Cultural Product in Denmark
item.content:encoded:
A new report by a Danish publication reveals that 007 First Light had a budget of over $200 million, making it Denmark’s most expensive cultural product.

IO Interactive had a great launch with 007 First Light, with the game selling 1.5 million copies in the first 24 hours. However, there is no way to know how these numbers help the developers without knowing how much money was spent to develop the game, and a new report seemingly reveals the budget.

007 First Light Reportedly Had a Budget of Over $200 Million

Spotted by IGN, a report by Denmark’s TV2 states that 007 First Light had a budget of 1.3 billion Danish krone, which comes up to around $202 million. The release has come as a major milestone for Denmark’s gaming industry, marking 007 First Light as “the most expensive cultural product in Denmark ever”, according to Niels Wetterberg, director of Games Denmark. There is also a comparison chart which shows the budget of Denmark’s most notable films, TV shows, and music videos, and 007 First Light’s cost is dramatically higher.

For those unaware, 007: First Light is a new, critically acclaimed espionage action-adventure game developed by IO Interactive. The game follows the origins of James Bond and his journey to becoming 007. Players will explore a variety of locations around the world, driving iconic vehicles, utilizing various gadgets, and resorting to both fistfights and firepower to achieve their objectives. As Bond, players can also use their charm to navigate challenges and get things done. The title has also become the highest-rated James Bond game in the past 30 years.

In other news, 007 First Light features a lot of cameos, including someone who can be considered as TikTok royalty. What are your thoughts on 007 First Light’s budget being over $200 million? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Silent Hill: Townfall’s rating surfacing online ahead of the State of Play event. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: When is Warzone Shutting Down on PS4 and Xbox One? All Dates, Explained
item.content:encoded:
In May, it was revealed that a battle royale-based era would be ending in 2026, with Warzone being removed from PlayStation 4 and Xbox One platforms. The paradigm shift might be long overdue, but it effectively means that millions of gamers worldwide will be frozen out of the game, which first launched back in March 2020.

But the removal won’t be an overnight thing. The Warzone shutdown will take place over several months, and below, I’ve outlined the dates you need to know. If you’re wondering when Warzone is shutting down on PS4 and Xbox One, look no further than this guide.

When is Warzone Leaving PS4 and Xbox One?

The grand sunsetting of Call of Duty: Warzone on PlayStation 4 and Xbox One starts on June 4th, which is when new downloads on these platforms will no longer be facilitated by Activision. This means that if you still have Warzone installed, you’re fine, but fresh players won’t be able to access the game.

Beyond that, on June 25th, the digital in-game store on the Warzone platform will be pulled from Xbox One and PS4 platforms, which means you won’t be able to buy any new content. You will still be able to access skins you’ve already bought.

Finally, when the first season of Modern Warfare 4 arrives later this year, which should be around the first or second week of November 2026, Warzone’s servers on PS4 and Xbox One will be shut down.

Why is Warzone Shutting Down on PS4 and Xbox One?

Activision has decided to leave the old generation behind with this year’s Call of Duty release, which we now know will be Modern Warfare 4. This next-generation first-person shooter will likely be too technically demanding and expensive to run on older hardware, which is fast becoming (if not considered so already) widely irrelevant.

As the launch of Modern Warfare 4 will bleed into the Warzone ecosystem, and the multiplayer-based shooter won’t be launching on PS4 and Xbox One, it stands to obvious reason that Warzone simply can’t persist on the older platforms.

This is why, after around November, you will not be able to play Warzone on PS4 or Xbox One.

Are you sad to see the back of Warzone on these older platforms? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov’s Lighthouse rework has been teased

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Silent Hill: Townfall Rating Surfaces Right Before PlayStation’s State of Play Event
item.content:encoded:
An ESRB rating for Silent Hill: Townfall has surfaced online, and it’s right before tonight’s PlayStation State of Play event, so a release date announcement could be close.

It has been some time since we heard about Silent Hill: Townfall. The last time we heard about it was during the ‘Transmission‘ event, when we saw some new details. However, now that the title’s hype has increased, players can’t wait for a release date announcement, and hopefully, Konami will announce one soon.

Silent Hill: Townfall Rated ‘M’ by ESRB

Spotted by MP1ST, a new rating for Silent Hill: Townfall has been listed on the ESRB’s website. The game has been rated ‘M’ for Mature and carries an age rating of 17+ due to blood, strong language, and violence. The game has been rated for Windows PC and PlayStation 5; no other platforms were listed.

The rating summary also provides players with information about the title, noting that it is a survival horror game in which you try to escape a fictional town in Scotland. It includes “realistic gunfire and large blood splatter effects,” which can affect the surrounding environment.

There are also two cutscene instances mentioned: one with a “close-up depiction of a character stabbing a body,” and one of “a man being eaten by a monster”. Players will also see characters mentioning the word “f**k” in the game. Other stuff was basic information, such as exploring locations, solving puzzles, using weapons, jump scares, and more.

In other news, Sony’s upcoming PlayStation State of Play event will be shown on big screens as a live fan theatrical event and will last 90 minutes. What are your thoughts on Silent Hill: Townfall’s rating surfacing online? Do you think a release date announcement is close? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a new Sly Cooper game being hinted at. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Escape from Tarkov’s Lighthouse Rework Teased by Nikita with New Images
item.content:encoded:
Nikita Buyanov, the head of the studio at Battlestate Games, has unveiled a series of images on social media that tease the incoming Lighthouse rework for Escape from Tarkov. The team has overhauled almost every map in Escape from Tarkov, the spearhead extraction shooter, but Lighthouse is the one that players agree needs the most drastic changes.

It’s arguably the most troublesome map in the game, as everything from the enemies that patrol it to the locations in which players spawn often comes into question.

Lighthouse Expansion Coming Soon

Four images were posted on social media by Nikita Buyanov, revealing a snippet of what’s coming in the big Lighthouse overhaul.

From new points of interest to a rejig of the tasks that occupy the map, Battlestate Games is expected to make some sweeping changes to Lighthouse, which has long been the most divisive map in the game. It was introduced in December 2021 along with the 0.12.12 patch, one of the most monumental and paradigm-shifting updates in Escape from Tarkov’s history.

It’s one of the toughest maps for a few reasons.

First, players must contend with Zryachiy and his goons if they want to venture towards the lighthouse itself, which is heavily mined and booby-trapped all the way in. Then, there’s a high chance that the Goons can spawn at one of several points on the map, adding more fuel to the boss-based fire.

The central point of the map is the water treatment facility, but getting there is a nightmare because it too is surrounded by mines and Rogues manning gun emplacements and boasting the ability to beam you from a drastically long way away.

Add that to a massive, expansive main road bursting with the potential for snipers to sit atop hills and PMC spawns that always feel too far from the action, and you’ve got a perfect storm of sorts.

The comments under Nikita’s post were mixed, but quite a few people have argued that the map should simply be removed from Escape from Tarkov entirely.

Do you think Lighthouse is the worst map in Escape from Tarkov? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Maverick Games has revealed Clutch

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: 12 New LEGO Pokémon Sets Officially Revealed
item.content:encoded:
LEGO and The Pokémon Company have officially revealed the next batch of sets releasing in August, with pre-orders now available.

After an initial launch in February, geared towards grown-ups more than the younger generation, kids of all ages will have plenty to choose from, with 12 new sets available for purchase, and an additional set available as a bonus.

New LEGO Pokémon Sets Officially Revealed

In a press release, LEGO and The Pokémon Company announced the latest sets for building ages six and over, which include fan favourites like Pikachu, Charizard, Squirtle, Gengar, and Umbreon.

The entire line-up is SMART Play enabled, meaning the LEGO Pokémon will be able to play back with interactive screen-free play. All of the sets are available to pre-order, with a release date of August 1st.

Sizes and prices of the sets vary, with the smallest and cheapest coming in at $14.99, while the most expensive, the Charizard vs Jolteon Ultimate Battle All-in-one set, consisting of 751 pieces, is priced at $119.99.

The full list of sets and descriptions are as follows:

  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Training House with Pikachu (72164) All-in-One set for ages 6+. This set includes a Pikachu-inspired tree house, training items, a Poké Ball, buildable sandwich, and more. Fans can get the chance to become a Pokémon Trainer as they nurture, feed, play, train and battle LEGO Pikachu. Includes 1 SMART Brick, 4 Tags, Figure and Charger.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Charizard vs. Jolteon Ultimate Battle (72167) All-in-One set for ages 8+ features Charizard and Jolteon in a fierce battle between the two iconic LEGO Pokémon. Look around and use the healing spray for support in the battle or have the Pokémon jump into a friendly training session together on the training grounds. Includes 2 SMART Bricks, 4 Tags, 2 Figures and Charger.  
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Berry Bash with Bulbasaur and Bidoof (72155) compatible set for ages 7+. In this set, children can roleplay one of the key steps of any great Pokémon Trainer – nurturing your LEGO Pokémon. The set comes with two Pokémon, the beloved Bulbasaur and Bidoof. Make a delicious berry smoothie in the juicer and feed the snack-hungry Pokémon!
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Trainer’s Buggy Adventure with Squirtle (72156) compatible set for ages 7+. Fans can take LEGO Squirtle out for a ride on the passenger seat in a beach buggy. The vehicle has 2 water stud shooters and a water stud storage compartment for Squirtle and the Trainer to extinguish fires at the veggie grill.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Charmander and Geodude’s Cavern Clash (72157) compatible set ages 6+. Fans can play out the ‘Trainer’s First Adventure’ with LEGO Charmander and Geodude. Charmander explores a cave with treasure and crystals. Stay alert and watch out for the angry Geodude.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Sprigatito, Fuecoco and Quaxly Battle (72158) compatible set for ages 8+ lets children play out stories for the three Paldean First Partner Pokémon. Each of the three LEGO Pokémon has a different type: Sprigatito is Grass-type, Fuecoco is Fire-type and Quaxly is Water-type. The spinning wheel can help choose your opponent and determine who gets to battle next!
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Jigglypuff Concert (72159) compatible set for ages 7+ features LEGO Jigglypuff. This Pokémon loves to sing and play music, with key elements like microphones, speakers and a music stage.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Drone Search for Mythical Mew (72161) compatible set for ages 8+ shows the Mythical Pokémon Mew. Children can go on searching for LEGO Mew, using a Poké Ball inspired drone to search for the ancient ruins where Mew hides.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Eevee and Lapras’s Treasure Hunt (72162) compatible set for ages 8+ features LEGO Eevee and Lapras off on a sea adventure to find the map to the shipwreck. Lowering the mast to lift a rock reveals a treasure chest full of coins and gems.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Mewtwo’s Lab Break (72163) compatible set for ages 10+ lets fans recreate a scene where LEGO Mewtwo breaks out of its lab tank. The set includes an adjustable lab tank, Mewtwo info screen, rare Master Ball and Mewtwo figure.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Umbreon vs. Garchomp Championship Battle (72165) compatible set for ages 10+ sees LEGO Umbreon and Garchomp together in a fun championship, battling to win the Poké Ball trophy. A giant Poké Ball is included.
  • LEGO® Pokémon™ SMART Play: Cubone and Gengar’s Spooky Showdown (72166) compatible set for ages 8+ shows LEGO Cubone trying to defeat Gengar to collect the treasure hidden underneath.

A gift with purchase set is also available for those who spend over $130 from the LEGO Pokémon category at the Pokémon Center, who will receive a Ditto as Squirtle: Move Night LEGO Smart Play Compatible set. The offer is available from today, June 2, until December 31, 2026, or while supplies last.

The announcement covers the sets that feature the Smart Brick LEGO mechanics, and does not include other sets understood to be on the horizon, which include Rayquaza, Munchlax, and Arcanine.

Which new LEGO Pokémon set has caught your eye? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, check out the winning team of the first Pokémon Champions VGC Regional, and read about the global sales numbers of the franchise.
